text:
Market today: Tech had one of its worst weeks in a year. The real driver is a cocktail of AI momentum doubts, geopolitical shock (U.S. striking Iran pushes oil up, risk-off mood), and chip-sector cracks shown by Micron's collapse. Your margin-heavy, chip-concentrated book is sitting right in the blast radius.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole compounds fast, stop at 336. PLTR is down 13% and also SELL-flagged — not a small loss to absorb on leverage. SPAL is down nearly 17% — remember it's a 2x leveraged SpaceX ETF that decays daily; it can crater fast. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — treat it as fragile.

Watch: Oil prices after the Iran strike — sustained spike hurts risk appetite and hits your tech names hard. Watch whether chip sentiment stabilizes after Micron's ugly week; SMH is your only green chip name right now.

Bottom line: You have two SELL-flagged positions bleeding double-digits on margin — that's where your attention belongs today.
